liveSaleCanary.input: 64 minecraft:cooked_porkchop sold and replaced by one debt-payment receipt
liveSaleCanary.duplicateSuppression: debt and deadline remained unchanged after later player inventory edits

incidentNotes: The menu now opens the real smart shipping bin as a vanilla six-row chest instead of ShippingBin v4 Inputs/Profits UI.
All 54 private persisted slots participate in sale planning and exactly-once compensation.

KubeJS denies the com.sun prefix, so the package allow was replaced by a strong exact-class allow for VirtualShippingBridge. The restarted server loaded the class and registered the inventory=54 scheduler successfully.

The disappearing menu was caused by a scheduler exception logger calling Rhino ConsoleJS.error with two arguments, leaving the owner record in PLANNING. Logging now uses one string argument, and menu acquisition safely reclaims an abandoned PLANNING token before opening.

Sales were retrying every five minutes but Rhino rejected block-scoped declarations inside the 54-slot loop as redeclarations. Loop-local declarations now use function-scoped var bindings compatible with the production Rhino runtime.
The production ItemStack wrapper does not provide a zero-argument hasNBT/hasTag method. Sale planning now reads the established KubeJS stack.nbt property directly.
The quality-perk multiplier comparison now normalizes the KubeJS value with String before JavaScript equality; direct-NBT, slot-53, offline-gate, and distinct-cycle policy QA cases pass.
The live debt-payment branch exposed Rhino block-const reuse for receipt; it now uses a function-scoped uniquely named variable and reads receipt NBT through the proven property surface.
The production canary sold 64 cooked porkchops for 3,072, reduced debt from 20,992 to 17,920 exactly once, emitted one receipt, returned to clean IDLE, and scheduled the next cycle.
Menu card hover entry and successful left-click now play the supplied UI sound at distinct pitch and volume; hover playback is edge-triggered to prevent per-frame repetition.
